title: AI Knows More Than You Think

modules.title: How Your Data Becomes AI Training
modules.sub: Tracing the path from your actions to AI models
modules.description: Learners explore how everyday online behavior — likes, searches, and purchases — gets collected and fed into AI training pipelines. You map out the journey from raw personal data to a finished model, making the abstract process concrete and personal.

modules.title: What Apps Are Actually Collecting
modules.sub: Reading the fine print most people skip entirely
modules.description: You dig into real app permissions and privacy policies to discover what data is harvested beyond the obvious. By comparing what apps claim to collect versus what they actually request, you develop a sharper eye for hidden data grabs.

modules.title: Your Digital Footprint Is Bigger Than You Know
modules.sub: Seeing the full scale of your online presence
modules.description: Learners piece together the many layers of their digital footprint, from metadata and location history to third-party trackers and data brokers. You'll likely be surprised by how much exists about you that you never consciously shared.

modules.title: How AI Profiles and Targets You
modules.sub: Understanding the profile built to predict your behavior
modules.description: This module reveals how AI systems stitch together your data into detailed behavioral profiles used for advertising, content ranking, and influence. You analyze real examples of targeted content and practice recognizing when you're being profiled and nudged.

modules.title: When Algorithms Make Decisions About You
modules.sub: High-stakes AI decisions that affect your real life
modules.description: You examine how algorithmic systems make consequential decisions — in hiring, credit, school admissions, and law enforcement — often without your knowledge. Learners evaluate case studies and consider what it means when a machine's output shapes your opportunities.

modules.title: Protecting Yourself Without Going Off-Grid
modules.sub: Practical privacy moves that fit your actual life
modules.description: Rather than demanding total digital withdrawal, this module gives you a toolkit of realistic, graduated steps to reduce your exposure and reclaim control. You choose and commit to a personal privacy action plan tailored to your habits and risk tolerance.

modules.title: Your Rights: What the Law Actually Says
modules.sub: Knowing the legal protections you already have
modules.description: Learners get a clear-eyed tour of the privacy laws that apply to them — from GDPR and CCPA to student and consumer protections — translated out of legalese. You practice using those rights by drafting a data deletion or access request you could actually send.

modules.title: Audit Your Own Digital Exposure
modules.sub: Running a real privacy audit on yourself right now
modules.description: In this capstone module, you conduct a structured audit of your own accounts, permissions, data broker listings, and privacy settings using a step-by-step framework. You leave with a completed exposure report and a prioritized list of actions to reduce your digital vulnerability.

synopsis: You've grown up online, which means AI systems have been learning from you â your clicks, your searches, your posts â for years. This course shows you what's actually been collected, how it's used to influence you, and what you can do about it.
rationale: Young adults are the most data-exposed demographic yet often the least informed about how that data shapes the AI decisions that affect their feeds, opportunities, and even job prospects.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can conduct a personal data audit, identify where your information is being used by AI systems, and take concrete steps to protect your privacy and understand your rights.
